data.title: Bragg Gaming Group: Cost Restructuring, AI-Driven Repositioning, and Market Refocus Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
Haywood analyst Gianluca Tucci reiterated a Buy rating on Bragg Gaming Group Inc yesterday and set a price target of C$7.50.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gianluca Tucci has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Bragg Gaming Group’s restructuring and strategic repositioning. He views the 12% global workforce reduction as a disciplined cost rationalization that is expected to deliver €4.5 million in recurring annual savings, far outweighing the roughly €1 million one-time charge in early 2026. In his assessment, this materially strengthens adjusted EBITDA, accelerates the path to sustainable profitability, and improves operating leverage. Tucci also notes that the leaner operating model should support healthier margins heading into the second half of 2026.

Tucci further bases his positive stance on Bragg’s decision to redeploy resources toward higher-growth opportunities and an AI-centric operating model. He believes the shift away from more resource-heavy legacy activities and toward AI-driven, scalable processes better aligns the company with its 2027 strategic vision. At the same time, he sees the redirection of investment from challenged jurisdictions such as the Netherlands to faster-growing markets like Brazil and broader LATAM as a catalyst for future expansion. Taken together, these measures are seen as enhancing financial flexibility, positioning Bragg to capture market share, and supporting his view that the shares offer attractive upside potential, justifying a Buy rating.

BRAG’s price has also changed dramatically for the past six months – from C$6.150 to C$3.100, which is a -49.59% drop .
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Groupon’s Turnaround Story: Improved Execution, Stronger Balance Sheet, and New Growth Phase Support Buy Rating
data.description:
Northland Securities analyst Bobby Brooks maintained a Buy rating on Groupon today and set a price target of $42.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bobby Brooks has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that, in his view, make Groupon one of the more attractive risk‑reward opportunities in his coverage. He points to clear evidence of a successful turnaround, including a return to billings growth, a sustained rebound in North American active customers after a long decline, and consistent double‑digit growth in local billings. He also highlights that management has moved from a period of uneven performance to a phase of steady execution, while significantly improving the balance sheet through a refinancing that extends most debt maturities to 2030 and removes restrictive covenants. This financial flexibility, together with the potential use of the company’s sizeable share repurchase authorization, supports his view of meaningful upside as the business enters a new growth phase.

Brooks further notes that there are still operational levers to pull, such as enhancing merchant support—particularly for smaller local businesses—which he believes represents “low‑hanging fruit” that can drive additional growth. He emphasizes his confidence in the current leadership team, which he sees as highly aligned and capable based on the progress already made. Finally, he expects management to increase its engagement with the investment community in 2026, improving investor awareness of the transformed story and potentially attracting more stable, long‑term shareholders. Taken together, these elements underpin his conviction that now is an attractive entry point for investors and justify his Buy rating on Groupon shares.

In another report released yesterday, Roth MKM also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $47.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: CytomX Therapeutics: Asymmetric Upside Ahead of Pivotal CX-2051 Colorectal Cancer Data Supports Reiterated Buy and $10 Target
data.description:
CytomX Therapeutics, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Mitchell Kapoor from H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on the stock and has a $10.00 price target.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mitchell Kapoor has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to CytomX’s lead colorectal cancer asset, CX-2051 (Varseta-M), and its upcoming Phase 1 expansion readout. He highlights that data expected in 1Q26 from roughly 100 patients come with attractive risk/reward, as earlier results in a smaller cohort have already shown response rates, disease control, and progression-free survival that exceed typical late-line colorectal cancer outcomes, even allowing for some deterioration as the dataset grows. Kapoor argues that if the larger study merely confirms the current signal—particularly the response durability and depth of tumor shrinkage at the 10 mg/kg dose—the market is likely to reassess the stock upward, removing the skepticism that current results reflect small-sample variability. This underpins his reiterated 12‑month price target of $10.

Kapoor also grounds his optimism in detailed feedback from key opinion leaders (KOLs), who view the interim efficacy as striking and see a realistic path to a pivotal, randomized 4th‑line trial against standard agents such as fruquintinib or regorafenib. These experts believe that response rates in the mid‑20% range or higher, coupled with progression-free survival of around five to six months and acceptable rates of severe diarrhea, would not only outperform current 4th‑line options by a wide margin but could also begin to challenge 3rd‑line standards. Kapoor emphasizes that even a scenario where the objective response rate falls to roughly half of the preliminary figure would still represent multiple‑fold improvement over existing late‑line therapies and support a clear registrational strategy. Only in a low‑efficacy downside case does he see a meaningful erosion of the long‑term thesis, leading him to conclude that the setup today offers limited downside with substantial potential upside, justifying his continued Buy stance.

According to TipRanks, Kapoor is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 4.8% and a 42.03% success rate. Kapoor covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Halozyme, Neurogene, and Crispr Therapeutics AG.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
